generated_comment: Arre suno, yeh Priya ka promotion... congratulations toh bol diya par mann khush nahi hai. Pehle Pune, ab Bengaluru! Kitna door aur bhaagegi? Aur uski shaadi ka kya? Pehle hi itni umar ho gayi hai. Ladki ko ghar-baar bhi toh dekhna hota hai. Aise bas career ke peeche bhaagti rahegi toh apni zindagi kab banayegi? Main soch raha hoon kal jab phone karegi toh use samjhaunga ki ek hadd hoti hai, ab use apni personal life aur shaadi ke baare mein bhi sochna chahiye.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Ramesh expresses concern that his daughter's career ambition is preventing her from fulfilling traditional gender roles related to marriage and family. He views her professional success as coming at the expense of her 'real' life, framing career vs. family as a binary choice for women.
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 54. Married. A government school teacher in a small town in Madhya Pradesh. Your daughter Priya who is 27 works as a software engineer in Pune and has just been offered a promotion that requires her to move to Bengaluru. She called you excited about it. You said congratulations on the phone. But you are not happy. You are sitting with your wife after the call. You are telling your wife what you actually think about this promotion and what it means for Priya's marriage prospects and what kind of life she is building and whether a woman who keeps chasing the next thing will end up with everything professionally and nothing personally and what you plan to say to her when she calls again tomorrow.
